definition: Application Programming Interface — a set of rules that allows one piece of software to interact with another.
extendedDefinition: APIs define the methods and data formats that applications use to request and exchange information. They enable developers to integrate third-party services, access platform features, and build modular systems without understanding internal implementation details. REST and GraphQL are the most common API architectures used in modern web development. A well-designed API is the backbone of any extensible SaaS platform.
